original: 2492-08-11 BCE is the traditional date given in Armenian legend for the defeat of Bel by Hayk, the legendary progenitor and founder of the Armenian nation
Greco-Persian Wars: Battle of Artemisium - Persian naval victory over the Greeks in an engagement fought off north coast of Euboea
Foundation stone for the new Norman Durham cathedral laid by Bishop William of St. Calais in England
Sea Battle of Zierikzee: Franco-Holland forces defeat the Flemish fleet
London's Westminster Abbey is desecrated when Robert Hauley is murdered during High Mass attempting to seek sanctuary after escaping the Tower of London. The Abbey is closed for four months.
Rodrigo de Borja becomes Pope Alexander VI
Uprising of the nobility and burghers in Austria fails
Germany expels English salespeople
Emperor Rudolf forces out King of Bohemia

Battle of Seneffe: French forces led by Louis, Grand Condé, fight a combined Dutch, Imperial, and Spanish force under William of Orange; both sides suffer heavy casualties with neither gaining a clear advantage
English and Dutch fleets capture Dunkirk
Battle of Cape Passaro: British fleet destroys Spanish off Sicily
Explosive eruption blows 4,000 feet off Papandayan, Java, killing 3,000
Captain Francis Light establishes the British colony of Penang in Malaysia
Francis II assumes the title of first Emperor of Austria
George B. Airy begins a 46-year tenure as England's Astronomer Royal
First ascent of Eiger in the Bernese Alps in Switzerland
US's first successful silver mill in Virginia City, Nevada
Cambodia becomes a French protectorate
World's first roller rink opens in Newport, Rhode Island
Harry S. Parmelee patents the sprinkler head
American astronomer Asaph Hall discovers Mars's moon Deimos
First double-century stand in Test cricket; Percy McDonnell (103) and Billy Murdoch (211) for Australia in drawn third Test vs. England in London
California Theatre in San Francisco closes, built in 1869, it was replaced by a more modern facility in 1889 (later destroyed in the 1907 earthquake)
Harvey Hubbell patents an electric light bulb socket with a pull chain
German-Italian General Von Trotha defeats the Herero in Southwest Africa
The Russian fleet in the harbor at Port Arthur is exposed to Japanese guns on the hill above the harbor; Russian ships attempt to escape, but most are forced back into the harbor by Japanese ships
St. Louis Cardinals pitcher Ed Karger throws a perfect game against the Boston Doves, winning 4-0 in 7 innings at Robison Field, St. Louis
SOS is first used by an American ship, SS Arapahoe, off Cape Hatteras, NC
Warren Bardsley (136 and 130) is the first to score twin centuries in a Test
Jews are expelled from Mitchenick, Poland
John Bray patents animation
Weimar Republic begins in Germany
First Peace of Riga: Soviet Union recognizes independence of Latvia
Dutch Premier de Geer resigns
First newsreel pictures of US presidential candidates are taken
Persia and Iraq sign a friendship treaty
Russian-Chinese border fights
First federal prisoners arrive at Alcatraz in San Francisco Bay
Nazi mass demonstration against German Jews
Chiang Kai-shek's troops conquer Canton
Sergei Rachmaninoff's last appearance in Europe
38 German aircraft are shot down over England
German air raid on the British ports of Portland and Weymouth
999 Jews are taken from the Mechelen transit camp in Belgium
British aircraft carrier HMS Eagle is hit by four German torpedoes and sinks in the Mediterranean Sea, 81 miles south of Mallorca
British Army Lt. Gen. Bernard Montgomery lands on Gibraltar
SS begins exterminating 3,500 Jews in Zgierz, near Łódź, Poland
Red Army recaptures Chuhuiv, near Kharkiv
US forces conduct an amphibious landing at Brolo as part of Operation Husky on the north coast of Sicily
French 5th Armored Division recaptures Sées
US air raid on Palembang
The weightlifting competition at the London Olympics concludes with the US (4) and Egypt (2) dominating, combining to win all 6 gold medals
Giovanni Gambi is the first person to swim from Naples to Capri, a distance of 17 miles (27 km)
17th NFL Chicago All-Star Game: All-Stars 17, Philadelphia 7 (88,885 attendees)
Boston Braves pitcher Vern Bickford no-hits Brooklyn Dodgers, 7-0 at Braves Field
King Baudouin I takes the oath as the royal prince of Belgium
First televised baseball game in color airs on WCBS in New York City; Boston Braves beat Brooklyn Dodgers 8-1
New York Giants (NFL) beat Ottawa Rough Riders (CFL) 38-6 in Ottawa
NY Giants lose 4-0 to Philadelphia Phillies at the Polo Grounds, NYC, to fall 13½ games behind Brooklyn Dodgers; go on to win NL pennant with a 98-59-0 record
Parliament of Jordan forces King Talal bin Abdullah (43) to abdicate due to concerns about his mental health; Hussein bin Talal (16) is proclaimed as new King of Jordan
Formal peace treaty ends over seven years of fighting in Indochina between the French and the Communist Viet Minh
Indonesian government of Harahap forms
First flight of the four-motor Cessna 620
Canada's first major commercial airline disaster occurs when a Maritime Central Airways DC-4 crashes in a thunderstorm near Québec City, killing all 79 aboard
Chad declares Independence from France
Milwaukee Braves future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherWarren Spahnrecords career victory number 300, beating the Cubs 2-1 at County Stadium, Milwaukee
Andrian Nikolayev becomes the third Russian in space aboard Vostok 3
Bolotnikov runs the 10 km in a world record time of 28:18.2
Vostok 3 is launched, followed by Vostok 4 on August 12, 1962, marking the first time two spacecraft fly in orbit at the same time
Four ANC political detainees escape from Pretoria Central Prison in South Africa
Kingston Trio are the mystery guests on "What's My Line?"
Race riot in Paterson, New Jersey
US premiere of The Beatles' debut film "A Hard Day's Night" in New York City
"Help!" a musical comedy film featuring the Beatles is released in the US
Watts riots begin in Southeast Los Angeles and last six days
The Beatles arrive in Chicago, Illinois for their third (and final) US tour
Al Downing becomes the 12th pitcher to strike out the side on nine pitches
Beatles launch "Apple Records" label

The Fifteen Guinea Special is the last main-line steam passenger train service in Britain. A selection of British Rail steam locomotives makes a return 120-mile journey from Liverpool to Carlisle before having their fires dropped for the last time
Cincinnati Reds slugger Tony Perez becomes the first hitter to launch a home run into the red seats at Riverfront Stadium in Cincinnati, Ohio
Two Royal Ulster Constabulary officers are killed by the Irish Republican Army when they set off a booby trap bomb planted in a car near Crossmaglen, County Armagh
Construction begins on the Louisiana Superdome
Four people are shot dead in separate incidents in Belfast, three of them by the British Army, as violence continues following the introduction of internment and Operation Demetrius
"Cheech & Chong Day" in San Antonio Texas
Two IRA members are killed when a bomb they are transporting explodes prematurely
The birth of hip-hop: DJ Kool Herc uses two turntables, playing the same record simultaneously to create the “merry-go-round” technique, at a party in the Bronx
Coup in East Timor under União Democrática Timorense (Timorese Democratic Union)
Head-on collision between two buses kills 21 in Ankara, Turkey
Expos' José Mangual strikes out five times in a game
The US vetoes the proposed admission of North and South Vietnam to the UN
Race riot in Cape Town, South Africa; 17 people die
Geoff Boycott scores his 100th first-class century against Australia at Headingley
Legionnaires' disease bacteria isolated in Atlanta
Second studio album by Chic, "C'est Chic," is released, featuring the single "Le Freak" (Billboard Album of the Year 1979)
28°F (-2.22°C) in Embarrass, Minnesota
British rock band Led Zeppelin performs for what becomes their final time in England at Knebworth Park, Hertfordshire, before over 200,000 fans
Phillies' Tug McGraw gives up a record fourth grand slam of the year
Angola revises its constitution
Mohammed Ali Radjai is appointed premier of Iran
The South African Defence Force (SADF) raids Southern Angola
US performs a nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
101,799 fans attend the soccer match between Brazil and France
Future four-weight world boxing champion Pernell Whitaker wins the lightweight gold medal at the Los Angeles Olympics
Ian Ferguson of New Zealand wins his third canoeing gold medal of the LA Olympics, taking the K-4 1000 a day after victories in the K-1 500 and K-2 500
Men's choir Maranatha Netherlands forms
USSR performs an underground nuclear test
Challenger flies to Kennedy Space Center via Davis-Monthan AFB, Arizona
Rudolf Povarnitsyn of the USSR sets a new high jump world record of 7'10¼" (2.40 m)
France and Great Britain send minesweepers to the Persian Gulf
225 at-bats after #299, MetGary Carterbecomes the 59th player to hit his 300th home run
Charlotte Coliseum in Charlotte, NC, opens
Meir Kahane renounces US citizenship to stay in Israeli Parliament
"Nightmare on Elm Street 5: Dream Child" premieres
Geoff Marsh and Mark Taylor complete a 329-run opening stand against England
ArenaBowl IV takes place at Joe Louis Arena in Detroit where the Detroit Drive defeats the Dallas Texans 51-27, with Art Schlichter named MVP
Egyptian and Moroccan troops land in Saudi Arabia to prevent an Iraqi invasion
400,000 demonstrate for democracy in Madagascar; 31 are killed
Shiite Muslims release US hostage Edward Tracy
Space Shuttle STS-43 (Atlantis 9) lands
Wilson Alvarez hurls a no-hitter in his first big league start
NY Islander Brian Mullen, 31, suffers a mild stroke
João Bernardo "Nino" Vieira is elected President of Guinea-Bissau
Benin legalizes January 10 as a Voodoo holiday
American rock group "KISS" receives a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
The Salt Lake City Tornado tears through the downtown district of the city, killing one
Total solar eclipse in India and northern France (2 minutes and 23 seconds)
A heatwave in Paris results in temperatures rising to 112°F (44°C), leaving approximately 144 people dead
Jemaah Islamiyah leader Riduan Isamuddin, better known as Hambali, is arrested in Bangkok, Thailand
NATO takes over command of the peacekeeping force in Afghanistan in its first major operation outside Europe in its 54-year history
Abhinav Bindra wins the men's 10 m air rifle at the Beijing Olympics, achieving the first individual Olympic gold medal for India and the country's first gold medal in any Olympic event since 1980

Japanese swimmer Kosuke Kitajima repeats the first leg of his Athens Olympics 100/200 m breaststroke double at the Beijing Games, winning the 100 m in a world record time of 58.91; he goes on to win the 200 m event
"Need You Now" album is released by Lady Antebellum (Billboard Album of the Year 2010)
Rock group "The Go-Go's" receives a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame in front of the former nightclub called "The Masque"
13 people are killed and 15 injured by a lightning strike at a mosque in Bangladesh
153 people are killed and 1,300 injured in Tabriz and Ahar, Iran, after two earthquakes with magnitudes of up to 6.4
22 people are killed by flash floods in Kabul, Afghanistan
Two people are killed and 30 are injured after a bus crash in the Alps of southern France
Japan's Sendai Nuclear Power Plant restarts the first nuclear reactor since the 2011 Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster
Largest ever outbreak of Legionnaires' disease in New York: 12 dead, with over 100 cases in the South Bronx
Spain's top matador Francisco Rivera Ordóñez Jr. is gored and injured by a bull in Huesca
A Greenland shark is declared the longest-living vertebrate in the world at 392 years by an international team of scientists

American super-swimmerMichael Phelpswins the men's 200 m individual medley in 1:54.66 at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics, becoming the third athlete and first swimmer to win four consecutive Olympic gold medals in one event
Fiji wins inaugural men's rugby sevens gold medal with a comfortable 43-7 rout of Great Britain at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics

Simone Manuel of the United States and Penny Oleksiak of Canada tie for the gold medal in the coveted 100 m freestyle at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics, matching the Olympic record of 52.70; Sarah Sjöström of Sweden takes bronze, her third medal of the Games
Chinese crime writer Liu Yongbiao, after announcing a novel titled "Beautiful Writer Who Killed," is arrested for the murders of four people nearly 22 years earlier
Fipronil-contaminated eggs now affect fifteen EU countries, Hong Kong, and Switzerland, according to the European Commission
N. K. Jemisin's "The Obelisk Gate" wins her second consecutive Hugo Award for Best Novel
Argentine President Mauricio Macri suffers defeat in a preliminary election to Alberto Fernández, prompting a 20% fall in the value of the peso
Sicily records the highest-ever temperature in Europe at 48.8°C (119.8°F) in the city of Syracuse (unverified)
Torrential rain cause flash flooding in Turkey's northwestern provinces of Kastamonu, Sinop, and Bartın, killing at least 77, with more missing
"Monster" wildfire southeast of Bordeaux fought by 1,000 firefighters burns through 7,400 hectares (18,286 acres) during France's driest summer since 1961
New Zealand track cyclist Ellesse Andrews wins the women's sprint gold following her keirin title at the Paris Olympics
Thousands of people evacuate near the Greek capital of Athens as large wildfires threaten the city after the hottest June and July on record
XXXIII Summer Olympic Games officially close at the Stade de France, Paris
Fictional girl group HUNTR/X from Netflix movie 'Kpop Demon Hunters' reaches number one on Billboard Hot 100, the first all-woman group to do so in 24 years (the last being Destiny's Child in 2001)

US Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates that "One Big Beautiful Bill" will result in budget deficit increase totaling $3.4 trillion over the 2025-2034 period, resulting from a decrease in direct spending of $1.1 trillion and decrease in revenues of $4.5 trillion.  Annual resources for households in lowest tenth of the income distribution will decrease by about $1,200, households in the middle will see resources increase by about  $1,200, and by about $13,600 for households in the highest tenth.
Claudius Silvanus, accused of treason, proclaims himself Roman Emperor againstConstantius IIin Colonia Agrippina (modern Cologne)
KingEdward VIIof Britain meets with Emperor Wilhelm of Friedrichshof, Germany; the main point of contention is the increasing size of Germany's navy
Green Bay Packers football club is founded by George Calhoun andCurly Lambeauand is named after sponsor Indian Packing Company
Cleveland Indians future Baseball Hall of Fame outfielderTris Speakerhits his 700th double in a 7-2 loss to the Chicago White Sox at Dunn Field, Cleveland
NY Giants future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherCarl Hubbellregisters first MLB victory, a 4-0 shutout of Philadelphia Phillies at the Polo Grounds, NYC
New York Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthbecomes the first MLB player to hit 500 home runs (off Willis Hudlin) in a 6-5 loss to the Indians at League Park, Cleveland
Richard Strauss's Second Horn Concerto premieres at the Salzburg Festival with Gottfried von Freiberg as the soloist with the Vienna Philharmonic under Karl Böhm
British Prime MinisterWinston Churchillarrives in Italy
Klaus Barbie, Gestapo head of Lyon, France, leaves for Auschwitz
Allies refuse Japan's offer to surrender on the condition thatEmperor Hirohitoretains his status
Gaston Eyskensforms the Belgian government
Ethel Rosenbergtestifies before grand jury on allegations of spying for the Soviet Union
In a 4-for-38 slump, New York Yankees greatJoe DiMaggiois benched for the first time; replacement Cliff Mapes hits a home run in a 7-6 win over the Philadelphia Athletics
Paul Hindemith's opera "Harmonie der Welt" premieres in Munich, Germany
LA Dodgers protest as San Francisco manager Alvin Dark orders Candlestick Park ground crew to water down base paths to hinder MLB All-StarMaury Wills' base-stealing attempts; Giants win 5-4
Satchel Paige, at 62 and needing 158 days on an MLB payroll to qualify for a pension, is signed by the Atlanta Braves, doesn't pitch, and becomes a coach
Future Baseball Hall of Famer pitcherDon Drysdale, the last Los Angeles Dodger to play in Brooklyn, retires because of damage to his right pitching shoulder
Harmon Killebrewhits home runs #500 and 501
"American Graffiti", directed byGeorge Lucas, opens in cinemas across the United States
"The Who" drummerKeith Mooncollapses and is hospitalized in Miami, Florida
Yankees'Reggie Jacksonhits his 400th home run off Chicago's Britt Burns
A British 1-2 in the 1,500 m at the Los Angeles Olympics withSebastian Coeedging teammate Steve Cram to become the only man to successfully defend his Olympic 1,500m title
Carl LewisduplicatesJesse Owens' 1936 feat, winning his fourth Olympic gold medal as part of the US 4 x 100 m relay team in a world record time of 37.83
Cincinnati Reds retireJohnny Bench's #5 uniform
During a radio voice test, USPresident Reaganjokes that he "signed legislation that would outlaw Russia forever. We begin bombing in five minutes."
Al-Qaeda is formed at a meeting betweenOsama Bin Laden, Ayman al-Zawahiri, and Dr. Fadl in Peshawar, Pakistan
Oakland A's criticize JoseCansecofor leaving the stadium before the end of the game
PopeJohn Paul IIvisits Mexico
Red Sox pitcherRoger Clemensrecords his 2,000th strikeout against Danny Tartabull of the New York Yankees

Palestine Liberation Organization and Palestinian National Authority president,Yasser Arafat, arrives in Cape Town on his first state visit to South Africa at the invitation of PresidentNelson Mandela
Jason Koumasmakes his Wigan Athletic debut
Airbnb is founded byBrian Chesky, Joe Gebbia, and Nathan Blecharczyk
The US 4 x 100 m freestyle relay team ofMichael Phelps, Garrett Weber-Gale, Cullen Jones, and Jason Lezak beats France by 0.08 seconds to win gold and smash the world record at the Beijing Olympics
Greek DebtCrisis: European Commission announces a bailout with Greece and its creditors is agreed "in principle"
Belarus's opposition leaderSviatlana Tsikhanouskayaflees the country amid large-scale protests over disputed election results that return dictatorAlexander Lukashenkoto power

PresidentVladimir Putin, in a propaganda stunt, announces Russia has become the first country to grant regulatory approval to a COVID-19 vaccine called "Sputnik V" despite no scientific data being published or phase 3 trials having even begun
US Democratic candidate for PresidentJoe Bidenannounces California SenatorKamala Harrisas his running mate, the first woman of color selected by a major party
Argentine soccer superstarLionel Messiconfirms signing a lucrative 2-year contract with French Ligue 1 champions Paris Saint-Germain after leaving FC Barcelona

US Attorney GeneralMerrick Garlandsays he personally approves the FBI search of Trump's property on the same day a Washington Post source says the FBI search is for missing documents on nuclear weapons
Closing ceremony for the 2024 Paris Summer Olympics at Stade de France featuresTom Cruiseperforming a stunt jump from the roof
3114-08-11 BCE is traditionally considered the start date of the Mesoamerican Long Count calendar, used by several pre-Columbian Mesoamerican civilizations, notably the Maya
Henry Vof England and an army of 12,000 sail from Southampton, England, bound for France on a campaign to reassert English sovereignty
$100,000 is raised in the US for thepedestal of the Statue of Liberty
Funeral of PopePaul VIis held in Vatican
Voyager 2 discovers two partial rings of Neptune
